sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Follow-up on poorly controlled blood sugars and medication review.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports that his sugars are not very well controlled, and he is here to review his medications. He currently takes two milligrams of glimepiride twice a day, and he has two pills of glimepiride at home. He reports eating less pasta and wheat bread, but not consistently. He drinks club soda and prefers seltzer water. He reports foot pain and numbness, attributing it to peripheral neuropathy, and uses a foot scrub to manage it. He has cataracts and had cataract surgery a month ago, with a prescription from Dr. Sanchez. He's been smoking and is not ready to quit. He reports coughing due to congestion.
Findings
Physical exam reveals carotids are normal, pulse is regular, lungs are clear, and extremities are without edema. The patient's nails are thickened and mildly dry. There is decreased sensation in the tips of the first and second toes. Blood pressure was 140/60 at home. The patient's hemoglobin is 8.2.
Assessment
The patient presents with poorly controlled diabetes, peripheral neuropathy, hypertension, and hyperlipidemia. He is also experiencing foot pain and has a history of smoking.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: We will recheck the patient's blood work today. I want you to increase the glimepiride to four milligrams twice a day. Continue the metformin, metoprolol, lisinopril with hydrochlorothiazide, and amlodipine. Monitor for low sugars, such as weakness or sweating. I'm going to write a new prescription for four milligrams of glimepiride twice a day. Continue the atorvastatin. I want you to quit smoking. Follow up in four months.
